reply: The library's got pretty good hours! On weekdays it's open from 8 AM all the way to 10 PM, and on Saturdays from 8 AM to 4 PM. During exam season they extend weekday hours to midnight, which is super helpful. Just note it's closed on public holidays.

patterns: how do i get to the library
reply: The main library (Lending and Reference) is at the apex of the Greenhill campus, opposite CBG bank, across the car park. There's also a Law Library on the top floor of the Law Faculty building if you need legal resources specifically.
You can reach the library at gimpalibrary@gimpa.edu.gh for general questions. If it's specifically about Turnitin, use info_library@gimpa.edu.gh instead. Their phone is +233-(0) 501620138.

Here are the key numbers to have saved:

Security (24/7): 030-274-6882
Campus Clinic: 030-274-6001
Student Support: 030-274-6830
Main Switchboard: 030-274-6000

Security is available around the clock, so don't hesitate to call if you need help.
The Campus Clinic is on the main GIMPA Greenhill campus. If you need urgent care or first aid, call them at 030-274-6001. They're your first stop for any health issues on campus.

GIMPA has Wi-Fi across campus — just use your student credentials to connect. If you're having trouble with the password or connection, swing by the IT office in the School of Technology building on any weekday between 8 AM and 5 PM.

To apply to GIMPA, head to www.gimpa.edu.gh/admissions and go through the admissions portal. Application fees are GHc 200 for Bachelor's/Diploma, GHc 250 for Master's, GHc 360 for LLM, and GHc 450 for PhD. Requirements vary by programme, so it's worth checking the specific page for what you're interested in. You can also call the admissions office on +233-(0) 501620138 if you have questions.
Yes! The September 2026 intake is currently open. You can apply at www.gimpa.edu.gh/admissions. Don't wait too long — these spots fill up. Call +233-(0) 501620138 if you need help with the process.
The main entrance is GIMPA Junction (Main Gate), right off the Legon Bypass. That's the one most people use.
Greenhill Cafeteria is the main spot to grab food on campus. It's a popular hangout between classes!

The academic year runs in two semesters — First Semester from September to January, and Second Semester from February to June. Exams are usually in December/January and May/June. Registration typically opens about 4 weeks before each semester starts. The full 2025-2026 academic calendar is on the GIMPA website.

The big ones to know: academic integrity is taken seriously here — plagiarism or cheating can get you a course failure, suspension, or even dismissal. If there's ever a disciplinary issue, there's a proper process with a hearing and the right to appeal. Also, orientation is compulsory for fresh students. The full Student Handbook and Plagiarism Policy are on gimpa.edu.gh/policies.

GIMPA — the Ghana Institute of Management and Public Administration — was founded in 1961 as a joint Ghana Government and UN project. It started as the Institute of Public Administration to train civil servants, and has grown into one of West Africa's leading management and public administration universities. It's been around for 65 years now (GIMPA@65!) and has over 6,000 students across four campuses.
GIMPA has four campuses: the main Greenhill Campus in Accra (off the Legon Bypass), plus campuses in Kumasi, Takoradi, and Tema. Most undergraduate programmes are based at the Greenhill Campus.
You can reach GIMPA on these numbers: +233-(0) 501620138, +233-(0) 332095432, +233-(0) 302908076, or +233-(0) 302-404664. For email, it's info@gimpa.edu.gh. The website is www.gimpa.edu.gh.
The student portal (MIS Web) is at ienabler.gimpa.edu.gh — that's where you check your results, register for courses, and manage your academic records. Use your student credentials to log in.
GIMPA's Learning Management System (LMS) is at lms.gimpa.edu.gh. That's where you'll find your course materials, assignments, and online learning resources. Log in with your student credentials.

Your GIMPA student email is a Google account — you access it through accounts.google.com using your GIMPA-issued email address. If you haven't set it up yet, check with the IT office in the School of Technology building.
The official lecture and exam schedule is available at max-ease-manager.vercel.app. You can also check your personal timetable right here in the Uni-Assist app!

GIMPA has a Counselling Unit (GCU) that offers free, confidential support — individual counselling, career guidance, group sessions, and more. To book an appointment, go to scheduler.gimpa.edu.gh/ea or email gcu@gimpa.edu.gh. Both in-person and virtual sessions are available. Don't hesitate to reach out — it's completely confidential.

The Office of the Dean of Students (ODS) coordinates internship and industrial attachment opportunities. They also run pre-internship preparation workshops and CV clinics to help you get ready. Reach out to the Student Affairs office to find out what's currently available.

The Office of the Dean of Students runs CV Clinic and Resume Writing Workshops to help you put together a strong application. It's a free service for GIMPA students — check with the Student Affairs office for the next session.

There are several active student associations at GIMPA: the Student Representative Council (SRC), GIMPA Christian Fellowship, Law Students Association, Public Students Association, and GIBSAN (GIMPA Business School Association Network). Joining one is a great way to build your network and develop leadership skills. Ask around on campus or check with the Dean of Students office.

GIMPA has a Sports Unit established in 2021 that manages all sports activities for students, staff, and the wider community. They have active teams including the GIMPA Hockey Queens. For info on joining a team or sports activities, check with the Sports Coordinator through the Dean of Students office.

GIMPA's graduation ceremony is called the Congregation. It's held annually and is a big deal — a proper celebration of everyone's hard work. To qualify, you need to have passed all your required courses, met your CGPA requirements, completed any thesis or project, and cleared all financial obligations. You'll need to apply to graduate even if you're not attending the ceremony in person.

For student loans, the Student Loan Trust Fund (SLTF) is the main option for Ghanaian students — apply through sltf.gov.gh. GIMPA also has an Endowment Fund that supports scholarships for brilliant but needy students. For more info on available financial support, check with the Registry or Student Affairs office.

The GIMPA Endowment was set up in 2022 to raise funds for scholarships, research, infrastructure, and professorships. GIMPA runs without government funding, so the endowment is really important. If you want to donate, you can do so through CBG (Account: 0148340100010) or Ecobank (Account: 1441002555073), both under 'GIMPA Endowment'. More info at gimpa.edu.gh/gimpa-endowment.

GIMPA Business School offers an MBA (general management) and an Executive MBA (EMBA) for experienced professionals. The MBA requires a Bachelor's degree with at least Second Class Lower. The EMBA is designed for executives with significant work experience. Both are highly regarded programmes. For current fees and intake dates, check gimpa.edu.gh/admissions.

GIMPA's Faculty of Law offers the LLB (Bachelor of Laws) undergraduate programme, as well as the LLM (Master of Laws) and a Post-First Degree LLB. The Law Library is on the top floor of the Law Faculty building. For admission requirements and fees, check gimpa.edu.gh/admissions.

GIMPA offers PhD programmes across all its schools, as well as the Doctor of Management (DMGT) — an executive-focused doctorate for senior managers. PhD admission requires a Master's degree, strong academic record, a research proposal, and an interview. Application fee is GHc 450. The DMGT admissions are currently open — check gimpa.edu.gh for details.

The Doctor of Management (DMGT) is GIMPA Business School's executive doctoral programme — it's designed for senior managers who want to do applied management research at the highest level. You need a Master's degree plus at least 5 years of senior management experience. Admissions are currently open! Check gimpa.edu.gh for details.

The School of Technology offers BSc Information Technology and BSc Computer Science at undergraduate level, plus MSc programmes in IT, Digital Forensics & Cybersecurity, and ICT, IT & Law at postgraduate level. Strong maths background is important for these programmes.

The School of Public Service and Governance offers BA Public Administration at undergraduate level, and MPA (Master of Public Administration), Master of Governance and Leadership, and Masters in International Relations and Diplomacy at postgraduate level. Great programmes if you're interested in government, policy, or public sector careers.

GIMPA welcomes international students! There's an Office of International Programmes and Development (OIPD) at oipd.gimpa.edu.gh that handles international admissions and exchange programmes. GIMPA also has partnerships with universities abroad — for example, GIMPA Law students can earn a UConn (University of Connecticut) LLM degree through a Ghana-US partnership.

GIMPA may accept credit transfers from accredited institutions on a case-by-case basis. You'd need to provide detailed transcripts and course descriptions from your previous institution. The maximum transferable credits are usually limited to ensure you spend enough time at GIMPA. Contact the Registry at 030-274-6000 to discuss your specific situation.

GIMPA has a mature student admission route for applicants who are at least 25 years old, have completed SHS/SSS or equivalent, can provide proof of age, and pass the GIMPA Mature Programme entrance examination. It's a great option if you didn't go the traditional route straight from secondary school.

Yes, GIMPA offers part-time study options, especially for postgraduate programmes. Evening classes are typically 6 PM to 9 PM on weekdays, and there are weekend sessions for executive programmes. It's designed to work around your job, so it's popular with working professionals.

GIMPA's Training & Consulting arm (GTC) offers short courses and certificate programmes in areas like Leadership, Strategic Management, HR, Project Management, and more. These are great for professional development without committing to a full degree. Check gtc.gimpa.edu.gh for what's currently on offer.

Hawa Yakubu Hostel is the student accommodation on campus. For availability and how to apply for a room, check with the Student Affairs office. Many students also find accommodation in the surrounding Achimota and Legon areas.

Orientation is compulsory for all fresh students at GIMPA — you can't skip it. It typically happens at the start of the first semester in September. Missing orientation without a valid reason could affect your admission status, so make sure you're there!

If you have a complaint or issue, the Office of the Dean of Students handles student complaints and can mediate academic challenges with the relevant schools. You can also go directly to the Registry. For sexual misconduct specifically, there's a formal Sexual Misconduct Policy — see gimpa.edu.gh/policies.

GIMPA publishes the Greenhill Newsletter with campus news, student achievements, and upcoming events. The latest issue is on gimpa.edu.gh/news-events. You can also follow GIMPA on Facebook (GIMPAGreenhill), Instagram (@gimpa_greenhill), and Twitter (@GIMPA_Greenhill) to stay updated.

You can follow GIMPA on: Facebook (GIMPAGreenhill), Instagram (@gimpa_greenhill), Twitter (@GIMPA_Greenhill), LinkedIn, and YouTube (@gimpa_greenhill). Good way to stay in the loop on events and news!

GIMPA runs an annual Hackathon — a coding and innovation competition where students build and compete to create groundbreaking solutions. It's a great event if you're into tech. Keep an eye on the GIMPA social media pages and notice boards for the next one.

Akwaaba Artiste Night is GIMPA's big welcome event for new students — live performances, music, and a great way to meet people. It's usually held at the start of the academic year. Definitely one to look forward to!
Every March, GIMPA celebrates Ghana Month with events, performances, and exhibitions honouring Ghana's culture and history. It's a really vibrant time on campus!

Yes, GIMPA is fully accredited by the National Accreditation Board (NAB) of Ghana. It's also an independent public tertiary institution established by an Act of Parliament (GIMPA Act, 2004 — Act 676), so your degree is fully recognised.
GIMPA has over 6,000 students enrolled across its four campuses (Accra, Kumasi, Takoradi, and Tema) for both undergraduate and graduate studies.
GIMPA has an online shop at gimpa.edu.gh/shop where you can buy official merchandise. Worth checking out if you want to rep the school!
The Main Administration Block is the central hub for administrative offices, including the Rector's office and the Registry. It's one of the main buildings on the Greenhill campus.
The Executive Conference Centre (ECC) is where GIMPA hosts conferences, seminars, and major events. It's one of the landmark buildings on the Greenhill campus.
The GIMPA Business School building is on the main Greenhill campus. It houses the Business School lectures and offices.
The Graduate Lecture Block is on the Greenhill campus and is used for postgraduate lectures and classes.

I can help you find your classroom! Tell me your course code (like SOT702B) or course name (like IS RESEARCH METHODS), and I'll tell you the room location. Building codes: LS = Lecture Hall, GB = Graduate Block, E = Education Block.
IS RESEARCH METHODS (SOT702B) is held in LS 104 (Lecture Hall 104) on the Greenhill campus.
NETWORKS AND TELECOMMUNICATIONS (SOT704B) is held in LS 104 (Lecture Hall 104) on the Greenhill campus.
DATA WAREHOUSING (SOT711B) is held in LS 111 (Lecture Hall 111) on the Greenhill campus.
E-BUSINESS (SOT713B) is held in LS 111 (Lecture Hall 111) on the Greenhill campus. Some sessions may be in L2 GF2.

patterns: business intelligence and data mining location
reply: BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE AND DATA MINING (SOT714B) is held in LS 111 (Lecture Hall 111) on the Greenhill campus.
DATABASE AND PROGRAMMING CONCEPTS (SOT707B) is held in LS 111 (Lecture Hall 111) on the Greenhill campus.
Intelligent Systems for Legal and Administrative Applications (ITL706) is held in GB 210 (Graduate Block, Room 210) on the Greenhill campus.
TAXATION AND TAX ADMINISTRATION (BUS407B) is held in E 104 (Education Block, Room 104) on the Greenhill campus.

patterns: current issues in public sector location
reply: CURRENT ISSUES IN PUBLIC SECTOR MGT (SPG406B) is held in E 104 (Education Block, Room 104) on the Greenhill campus.
STRATEGIC MANAGEMENT (SPG405B) is held in LS 104 (Lecture Hall 104) on the Greenhill campus.
COMMUNICATION II (LAS202B) is held in E 104 (Education Block, Room 104) on the Greenhill campus.
WAGES AND SALARIES ADMIN. (SPG304B) is held in E 101 (Education Block, Room 101) on the Greenhill campus.
PUBLIC FINANCE (SPG306B) is held in E 101 (Education Block, Room 101) on the Greenhill campus.
RESEARCH METHODS I (SPG307B) is held in E 101 (Education Block, Room 101) on the Greenhill campus.
ADMINISTRATIVE AND OFFICE MANAGEMENT (SPG303B) is held in E 101 (Education Block, Room 101) on the Greenhill campus.
LEADERSHIP ETHICS AND SOCIAL RESP. (SPG305B) is held in E 101 (Education Block, Room 101) on the Greenhill campus.
LS 104 is Lecture Hall 104 on the Greenhill campus. It hosts courses like IS RESEARCH METHODS (SOT702B), NETWORKS AND TELECOMMUNICATIONS (SOT704B), and STRATEGIC MANAGEMENT (SPG405B).

LS 111 is Lecture Hall 111 on the Greenhill campus. It hosts courses like DATA WAREHOUSING (SOT711B), E-BUSINESS (SOT713B), BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE AND DATA MINING (SOT714B), and DATABASE AND PROGRAMMING CONCEPTS (SOT707B).
GB 210 is Room 210 in the Graduate Block on the Greenhill campus. It hosts Intelligent Systems for Legal and Administrative Applications (ITL706).

E 101 is Room 101 in the Education Block on the Greenhill campus. It hosts courses like WAGES AND SALARIES ADMIN. (SPG304B), PUBLIC FINANCE (SPG306B), RESEARCH METHODS I (SPG307B), ADMINISTRATIVE AND OFFICE MANAGEMENT (SPG303B), and LEADERSHIP ETHICS AND SOCIAL RESP. (SPG305B).

E 104 is Room 104 in the Education Block on the Greenhill campus. It hosts courses like TAXATION AND TAX ADMINISTRATION (BUS407B), CURRENT ISSUES IN PUBLIC SECTOR MGT (SPG406B), and COMMUNICATION II (LAS202B).
Building codes: LS = Lecture Hall/Block, GB = Graduate Block, E = Education Block, L2 = Level 2. All these buildings are on the Greenhill campus.
L2 GF2 means Level 2, Ground Floor Room 2. It's used for some sessions of E-BUSINESS (SOT713B).
TBA means To Be Announced — the room assignment hasn't been finalized yet. Check the official lecture schedule at max-ease-manager.vercel.app or contact your department for updates.

patterns: where is political and legal settings
reply: POLITICAL AND LEGAL SETTINGS (SPG204B) is held in E 107 (Education Block, Room 107) on Tuesdays from 1pm-4pm and Wednesdays from 5pm-8pm.
MANAGEMENT INFORMATION SYSTEMS (SOT320B) is held in E 107 (Education Block, Room 107) on Wednesdays from 9am-12pm and in E 202 (Education Block, Room 202) on Fridays from 5pm-8pm.
STATISTICS (BUS208B) is held in E 202 (Education Block, Room 202) on Tuesdays from 5pm-8pm.

patterns: where is development of public administration
reply: DEVELOPMENT OF PUBLIC ADMINISTRATION (SPG205B) is held in E 202 (Education Block, Room 202) on Thursdays from 5pm-8pm.
AFRICAN STUDIES (LAS100B) is held in E 202 (Education Block, Room 202) on Mondays from 5pm-8pm.
E 107 is Room 107 in the Education Block on the Greenhill campus. It hosts courses like POLITICAL AND LEGAL SETTINGS (SPG204B) and MANAGEMENT INFORMATION SYSTEMS (SOT320B).

E 202 is Room 202 in the Education Block on the Greenhill campus. It hosts courses like STATISTICS (BUS208B), DEVELOPMENT OF PUBLIC ADMINISTRATION (SPG205B), AFRICAN STUDIES (LAS100B), POLITICAL AND LEGAL SETTINGS (SPG204B), and MANAGEMENT INFORMATION SYSTEMS (SOT320B).
ELECTRONIC CIRCUITS/DIGITAL ELECTRONICS (SOT210B/SOT015B) is held in GB 303 (Graduate Block, Room 303) on Thursdays from 9am-12noon.
DATABASE SYSTEMS (SOT616B) is held in Consultancy 1 (CS 101) on Saturdays from 8:30am-11:30am.
Project (SOT600B) is not scheduled and the location is TBA.
COMMUNICATION SKILLS II (LAS202B) is held in Consultancy 1 (CS 201) on Thursdays from 5pm-8pm.
DATABASE DESIGN I / DATABASE SYSTEMS / Principles of Database Systems (CSC201B/ICT201B/SOT004B) is held in PSMTP Auditorium on Tuesdays from 5pm-8pm.
PROBABILITY AND STATISTICS / INTRODUCTION TO PROBABILITY (SOT204B/SOT006B) is held in GB 303 (Graduate Block, Room 303) on Wednesdays from 5pm-8pm.

GB 303 is Room 303 in the Graduate Block on the Greenhill campus. It hosts courses like ELECTRONIC CIRCUITS/DIGITAL ELECTRONICS (SOT210B/SOT015B) and PROBABILITY AND STATISTICS/INTRODUCTION TO PROBABILITY (SOT204B/SOT006B).
Consultancy 1 (CS 101) is a classroom in the Consultancy Block on the Greenhill campus. It hosts DATABASE SYSTEMS (SOT616B) on Saturdays.
Consultancy 1 (CS 201) is a classroom in the Consultancy Block on the Greenhill campus. It hosts COMMUNICATION SKILLS II (LAS202B) on Thursdays.
PSMTP Auditorium is an auditorium on the Greenhill campus. It hosts DATABASE DESIGN I / DATABASE SYSTEMS / Principles of Database Systems (CSC201B/ICT201B/SOT004B) on Tuesdays.
